curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: You ever actually READ the back of a shampoo bottle? 'Lather, rinse, REPEAT.' Repeat?! How many times, you sadistic pricks?! There's no fucking exit clause! I've been in this shower since the NINETIES! My balls are RAISINS! Somebody has to tell these animals when to STOP before I drown in here screaming about conditioner!
quotes.speaker: Gilbert Gottfried
quotes.context: Riffing on everyday absurdities, a 2009 stand-up set
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the shrieking escalation over a tiny everyday thing is peak Gottfried. The tell: the rant has a clean three-act build, and the real Gilbert never met a structure he wouldn't blow up by the second sentence.

quotes.text: My agent called to say I tested through the roof with audiences aged nine to eleven. Nine to eleven! I said Stu, that's not a demographic, that's a goddamn birthday party. Twenty years in this business and my core fan base still can't legally be left home alone. You know what that does to a man's sex life? It makes it theoretical.
quotes.speaker: Garry Shandling
quotes.context: On his career, a 1994 late-night appearance
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the neurotic showbiz spiral and the self-deprecating sex-life button are Shandling's exact register. The tell: he'd have found a way to make himself the punchline twice; this one lets the agent take one of the hits.

quotes.text: If you're gonna do it, you might as well fuckin' put your name above the door. It's always been about me anyway.
quotes.speaker: Liam Gallagher
quotes.context: Oasis frontman, on going solo and releasing music under his own name rather than a band name, in his trademark blunt interview style (Vice/Noisey interview).

quotes.text: I once asked a Jesuit priest what was the best short prayer he knew. He said, "Fuck it," as in "Fuck it; it's in God's hands."
quotes.speaker: Anthony Hopkins
quotes.context: Anthony Hopkins recounting a Jesuit priest's answer when he asked for the best short prayer — an anecdote he has told publicly, including on Inside the Actors Studio.

quotes.text: My brother said be cool, just be cool. So I'm being cool. Meanwhile this fool's got a velvet rope around his REFRIGERATOR. A rope! On the fridge! In his own damn house! And he's looking at me sideways for wanting a sandwich like I came to rob the place. That was the night I learned famous people will lose their whole goddamn minds and still expect YOU to act normal.
quotes.speaker: Charlie Murphy
quotes.context: Telling a backstage story on a 2010 stand-up special
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the deadpan 'be cool, just be cool' build into an absurd celebrity detail is the Charlie Murphy True-Hollywood-Story rhythm. The tell: the real story would've named the man and the rope would've been the third-craziest thing that night.

quotes.text: I bought a smoke detector and the guy asked if I wanted the kind that talks. Talks? I don't need my ceiling negotiating with me at three in the goddamn morning. If there's a fire, beep. We are not gonna have a fucking dialogue about it. I trust the beep. The beep has never once lied to me, which puts it miles ahead of every landlord I have ever had.
quotes.speaker: Mitch Hedberg
quotes.context: His deadpan one-liners, a 2003 Comedy Central set
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the over-literal argument with a household object is the Hedberg engine. The tell: a real Hedberg line lands in one breath, and he'd never have tagged it with that landlord button.
